what_it_does: This file is a noticeboard request item that records an evidence-backed fact pack about OpenAI Workspace Agents, including official source anchors, API facts, implications for the Company fabric, candidate use cases, and follow-up evidence questions.
observations: It is a request-type item in the channel-memory noticeboard with full YAML frontmatter and markdown body.
It cites three official OpenAI developer-docs URLs under developers.openai.com/workspace-agents.
It documents the trigger endpoint POST https://api.chatgpt.com/v1/workspace_agents/{id}/trigger and the agtch_XXX public API trigger identifier format.
Authentication is described as a Workspace Agent access token in the Authorization bearer header.
It frames Workspace Agents as ChatGPT workspace agents that can be triggered from backend systems or automations outside the ChatGPT UI.
It advises treating Workspace Agents as triggerable ChatGPT-side workers rather than the core Company fabric.
Because trigger runs are asynchronous, it recommends requiring the agent to write results back into Vi/fabric via an MCP/tool/channel path.
It lists potential uses: scheduled summaries, external event intake, Slack/team-facing agent, lightweight workflow actor, trigger into Company fabric, and periodic checks writing summaries.
It ends with open questions about plan availability, MCP access, response retrieval API, permissions model, and a minimal pilot.
standouts: It provides a source-anchored fact pack with direct URLs and concrete API endpoint details for Workspace Agents.
It explicitly separates Workspace Agents from Codex, ChatGPT Agent, and Claude Code in the Company capability map.
It prescribes an asynchronous write-back integration pattern rather than expecting a synchronous usable result from the trigger call.
conventions: Follows the noticeboard item schema with id, address, type, source, state, title, author_session, channel, thread, timestamps, and history in YAML frontmatter.
Uses a structured markdown body with labeled sections: source anchors, facts, meaning for Company fabric, potential Company uses, and questions for later evidence passes.
Maintains a neutral, evidence-backed tone and records open questions separately.
concerns: The source URLs and refresh date (2026-06-23) cannot be verified from the file alone.
The term "Conceptv" appears in a question without being defined in this file.
Some content is forward-looking or speculative, such as potential uses and integration recommendations, rather than strictly sourced facts.
There are no inline citations or direct quotations from the official sources.
notes: A noticeboard request that captures an OpenAI Workspace Agents fact pack and spells out how the Company fabric should relate to them.
questions: What is "Conceptv" and how does it relate to the Company fabric?
What exactly is "Vi/fabric" and what does an MCP/tool/channel write-back path look like?
Are the listed OpenAI developer-docs URLs authoritative and current as of the stated refresh date?
What is the precise boundary of the "Company fabric" and how are capabilities formally recorded?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to provide an evidence-backed fact pack for Workspace Agents; the file does that and adds Company-fabric interpretation plus open questions, so the purpose matches with added contextual framing.
apparent_themes: Keeping external triggerable agents separate from the core fabric.
Evidence-driven decision making with explicit open questions.
intention_signals: It is trying to establish a shared, sourced understanding of OpenAI Workspace Agents so the team can decide whether and how to integrate them as external triggerable workers while preserving the core Company fabric.
novelty: It explicitly positions Workspace Agents as a distinct capability layer from Codex, ChatGPT Agent, and Claude Code, and prescribes an async write-back integration pattern for the Company fabric.
contribution: It contributes a structured, source-anchored fact pack and integration posture for Workspace Agents, including endpoint details, authentication method, async usage guidance, candidate use cases, and a set of follow-up evidence questions.
summary_for_embedding: This noticeboard item is an evidence-backed fact pack about OpenAI Workspace Agents, documenting official source URLs, the trigger endpoint and authentication scheme, guidance on treating them as external triggerable workers in the Company fabric, candidate use cases, and open questions.
intention_for_embedding: It was created to refresh and share an official-source understanding of Workspace Agents and to guide their potential integration with the Company fabric.
